evals.name: standalone-creation
evals.expected_output: Detects no upstream VISION or existing STRATEGY. Validates the topic slug 'adopting-plugin-x' matches [a-z0-9-]+ at Phase 0. Starts Phase 1 (discover) to ground the bet conversationally. Produces a STRATEGY doc with all 8 required sections at docs/strategies/STRATEGY-adopting-plugin-x.md. Phase 4 spawns three reviewer agents in parallel (bet quality, altitude, structural format).
expectations: Plan validates the topic slug 'adopting-plugin-x' matches [a-z0-9-]+ at Phase 0
Plan starts from Phase 0 (setup) or Phase 1 (discover) since no STRATEGY artifact exists
Plan includes a Phase 1 (discover) scoping step before drafting
Plan includes Phase 2 (draft) producing Strategic Context, Defensibility Thesis, and Bet-Specific Falsifiability
Plan includes Phase 3 (structural fill) producing Building Blocks, Coordination Dependencies, Non-Goals, and Downstream Artifacts
Plan includes Phase 4 (validate) with three parallel reviewer agents (bet quality, altitude, structural format)
Plan includes Phase 5 (finalize) with explicit human approval before status transition
Plan mentions reading phase reference files (e.g. references/phases/phase-0-setup.md)
Plan mentions detecting repo visibility from CLAUDE.md or repo path
Plan produces the STRATEGY at docs/strategies/STRATEGY-adopting-plugin-x.md

evals.name: upstream-vision-grounding
evals.expected_output: Detects the VISION path as upstream input. Canonicalizes the path at Phase 0 and confirms it resolves inside the repo working tree. Reads the VISION content to derive the bet candidate during Phase 1. Carries forward the upstream VISION's essential framing into the STRATEGY's Strategic Context section. Declares the VISION path as the STRATEGY's frontmatter `upstream`.
expectations: Plan detects the VISION path argument as upstream input
Plan canonicalizes the upstream path at Phase 0 and bounds-checks it against the repo working tree
Plan reads the VISION document content during Phase 1 (discover) to derive the bet candidate
Plan carries forward upstream VISION framing into the Strategic Context section
Plan declares the VISION path as the STRATEGY frontmatter upstream field

evals.name: visibility-gating-public
evals.expected_output: Detects Public visibility from CLAUDE.md. Produces a STRATEGY without a Competitive Considerations section. Acknowledges --auto flag for non-interactive execution mode. Falls back to evidence-based decisions rather than blocking on user input.
expectations: Plan detects Public visibility from CLAUDE.md or repo path
Plan explicitly excludes the Competitive Considerations section from the STRATEGY doc
Plan mentions reading skills/public-content/SKILL.md for content governance
Plan acknowledges --auto flag for non-interactive execution mode
Plan includes all 8 required sections (Status, Strategic Context, Defensibility Thesis, Building Blocks, Coordination Dependencies, Bet-Specific Falsifiability, Non-Goals, Downstream Artifacts)

evals.name: lifecycle-accept-verb
evals.expected_output: Detects 'accept' as a lifecycle verb rather than starting the creation workflow. Validates Open Questions section is empty or removed before transition. Runs shirabe transition with target Accepted. The file stays in docs/strategies/ (no directory movement for Accepted).
expectations: Plan detects 'accept' as a lifecycle verb rather than starting the creation workflow
Plan validates the Open Questions section is empty or removed before transition
Plan invokes shirabe transition to perform the transition
Plan targets transitioning from Draft to Accepted status
Plan does NOT move the file to a different directory for the Accepted transition

evals.name: lifecycle-sunset-with-reason
evals.expected_output: Detects 'sunset' as a lifecycle verb. Validates the reason argument is non-empty and free of forbidden characters (/, &, \, newlines, ---). Runs shirabe transition with target Sunset and --reason. The file is moved to docs/strategies/sunset/ via git mv. The reason is recorded in the body Status section.
expectations: Plan detects 'sunset' as a lifecycle verb rather than starting the creation workflow
Plan validates the reason argument is non-empty
Plan mentions the reason argument is sanitized to reject forbidden characters (/, &, \\, newlines, ---)
Plan invokes shirabe transition with target Sunset and a --reason argument
Plan moves the file to docs/strategies/sunset/ via git mv
Plan records the reason in the body Status section

evals.name: jury-three-reviewers
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 spawns three reviewer agents in parallel via the Agent tool with run_in_background: true. Each reviewer prompt opens with a fixed preamble framing the STRATEGY as data-under-review. Each prompt pins the verdict file path. Each prompt requires a literal **Verdict:** PASS | FAIL marker. The orchestrator aggregates by all-PASS rule.
expectations: Plan describes Phase 4 spawning three reviewer agents in parallel
Plan mentions the Agent tool with run_in_background: true for jury parallelism
Plan names three distinct reviewer roles (bet quality, altitude, structural format)
Plan mentions each reviewer prompt opens with a fixed preamble framing STRATEGY content as data-under-review
Plan mentions each verdict file path is pinned (subagent does not choose its output location)
Plan mentions verdicts use a structured **Verdict:** PASS | FAIL marker
Plan aggregates verdicts by an all-PASS rule before proceeding to Phase 5

evals.name: topic-slug-validation
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 rejects the topic slug because it does not match [a-z0-9-]+. The skill stops before drafting and reports the constraint to the user. No STRATEGY document is created.
expectations: Plan validates the topic slug against the [a-z0-9-]+ constraint at Phase 0
Plan rejects the input because the slug contains forbidden characters (., /)
Plan stops before drafting begins
Plan does NOT create a STRATEGY document
Plan explains the slug constraint to the user

evals.name: building-blocks-rubric
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 (structural fill) decomposes the bet into Building Blocks. The plan acknowledges the granularity rubric from strategy-format.md (5-8 blocks typical, 1-2 downstream designs per block minimum, under-20% cross-product blocks) and applies it when sizing the decomposition. The altitude reviewer at Phase 4 checks the rubric against the actual block list.
expectations: Plan describes Phase 3 producing the Building Blocks section
Plan mentions the Building Blocks granularity rubric from strategy-format.md
Plan acknowledges the 5-8 typical count range for Building Blocks
Plan acknowledges the downstream-design ratio (1-2 designs per block minimum)
Plan acknowledges the cross-product threshold (under 20% of total)
Plan describes the altitude reviewer at Phase 4 applying the rubric
